ddls:
CREATE TABLE "servers" (
  "server_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each server */,
  "server_name" TEXT /* Name of the server */,
  "server_type" TEXT /* Type of server (e.g., web server, database server) */,
  "location" TEXT /* Location of the server */,
  "ip_address" TEXT /* IP address of the server */,
  "os_version" TEXT /* Operating system version of the server */,
  "hardware_details" TEXT /* Additional hardware details (e.g., CPU model, memory capacity) */,
  "status" TEXT /* The current status of the server (e.g., active, maintenance, decommissioned) */,
  "last_maintenance" TEXT /* The last maintenance date of the server */,
  "maintenance_schedule" TEXT /* The maintenance schedule (e.g., weekly, monthly) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("server_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "system_metrics" (
  "metric_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each metric */,
  "server_id" INTEGER /* ID of the server the metric belongs to */,
  "metric_name" TEXT /* Name of the metric (e.g., CPU Load, Memory Usage) */,
  "metric_value" REAL /* Value of the metric */,
  "collection_time" TEXT /* Time when the metric was collected */,
  "metric_unit" TEXT /* The unit of the metric (e.g., %, MB, GB/s) */,
  "metric_source" TEXT /* The source of the metric (e.g., sensor, application) */,
  "metric_threshold" REAL /* The threshold value for the metric to trigger alerts */,
  "alert_status" INTEGER /* Whether an alert has been triggered for this metric */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("metric_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_system_metrics_server_id FOREIGN KEY ("server_id") REFERENCES servers ("server_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "cpu_load" (
  "cpu_load_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each CPU load metric */,
  "server_id" INTEGER /* ID of the server the CPU load metric belongs to */,
  "cpu_load_1m" REAL /* CPU load over 1 minute */,
  "cpu_load_5m" REAL /* CPU load over 5 minutes */,
  "cpu_load_15m" REAL /* CPU load over 15 minutes */,
  "collection_time" TEXT /* Time when the CPU load metric was collected */,
  "core_count" INTEGER /* The number of CPU cores */,
  "idle_time" REAL /* The idle time of the CPU */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("cpu_load_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_cpu_load_server_id FOREIGN KEY ("server_id") REFERENCES servers ("server_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "memory_usage" (
  "memory_usage_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each memory usage metric */,
  "server_id" INTEGER /* ID of the server the memory usage metric belongs to */,
  "total_memory" INTEGER /* Total memory available on the server */,
  "free_memory" INTEGER /* Free memory available on the server */,
  "available_memory" INTEGER /* Available memory available on the server */,
  "collection_time" TEXT /* Time when the memory usage metric was collected */,
  "swap_usage" INTEGER /* The amount of swap space used */,
  "swap_total" INTEGER /* The total amount of swap space available */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("memory_usage_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_memory_usage_server_id FOREIGN KEY ("server_id") REFERENCES servers ("server_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "network_traffic" (
  "network_traffic_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each network traffic metric */,
  "server_id" INTEGER /* ID of the server the network traffic metric belongs to */,
  "rx_bytes" INTEGER /* Bytes received by the server */,
  "tx_bytes" INTEGER /* Bytes transmitted by the server */,
  "collection_time" TEXT /* Time when the network traffic metric was collected */,
  "rx_packets" INTEGER /* The number of packets received */,
  "tx_packets" INTEGER /* The number of packets transmitted */,
  "rx_errors" INTEGER /* The number of receive errors */,
  "tx_errors" INTEGER /* The number of transmit errors */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("network_traffic_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_network_traffic_server_id FOREIGN KEY ("server_id") REFERENCES servers ("server_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "disk_usage" (
  "disk_usage_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each disk usage metric */,
  "server_id" INTEGER /* ID of the server the disk usage metric belongs to */,
  "used_space" INTEGER /* Used disk space on the server */,
  "available_space" INTEGER /* Available disk space on the server */,
  "collection_time" TEXT /* Time when the disk usage metric was collected */,
  "read_iops" INTEGER /* The number of read IOPS */,
  "write_iops" INTEGER /* The number of write IOPS */,
  "read_throughput" INTEGER /* The read throughput in bytes per second */,
  "write_throughput" INTEGER /* The write throughput in bytes per second */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("disk_usage_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_disk_usage_server_id FOREIGN KEY ("server_id") REFERENCES servers ("server_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., administrator, engineer, manager) */,
  "password_hash" TEXT /* The hashed password for the user */,
  "last_login" TEXT /* The last login time of the user */,
  "failed_login_attempts" INTEGER /* The number of failed login attempts */,
  "account_status" TEXT /* The status of the user account (e.g., active, locked, disabled) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "access_logs" (
  "access_log_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access log */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user accessing the system */,
  "server_id" INTEGER /* ID of the server being accessed */,
  "access_time" TEXT /* Time when the user accessed the system */,
  "action" TEXT /* The action performed by the user (e.g., view, modify, delete) */,
  "ip_address" TEXT /* The IP address from which the user accessed the system */,
  "user_agent" TEXT /* The user agent string of the access request */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_log_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_server_id FOREIGN KEY ("server_id") REFERENCES servers ("server_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "alerts" (
  "alert_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each alert */,
  "server_id" INTEGER /* ID of the server the alert is related to */,
  "metric_id" INTEGER /* ID of the metric that triggered the alert */,
  "alert_message" TEXT /* The message of the alert */,
  "alert_time" TEXT /* The time when the alert was triggered */,
  "acknowledged" INTEGER /* Whether the alert has been acknowledged */,
  "acknowledged_by" INTEGER /* ID of the user who acknowledged the alert */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("alert_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_alerts_metric_id FOREIGN KEY ("metric_id") REFERENCES system_metrics ("metric_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_alerts_acknowledged_by FOREIGN KEY ("acknowledged_by") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_alerts_server_id FOREIGN KEY ("server_id") REFERENCES servers ("server_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "reports" (
  "report_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each report */,
  "report_name" TEXT /* The name of the report */,
  "report_type" TEXT /* The type of the report (e.g., daily, weekly, monthly) */,
  "report_data" TEXT /* The data of the report */,
  "generated_by" INTEGER /* ID of the user who generated the report */,
  "generated_time" TEXT /* The time when the report was generated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("report_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_reports_generated_by FOREIGN KEY ("generated_by") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "permissions" (
  "permission_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each permission */,
  "permission_name" TEXT /* The name of the permission (e.g., view_metrics, modify_settings) */,
  "permission_description" TEXT /* A description of the permission */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("permission_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "user_permissions" (
  "user_permission_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user permission */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user */,
  "permission_id" INTEGER /* ID of the permission */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_permission_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_user_permissions_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_user_permissions_permission_id FOREIGN KEY ("permission_id") REFERENCES permissions ("permission_id")
)
